stories.title: How the Iran war is wasting American resources
Iran war enters its fourth week with no clear end in sight
stories.description: As the war in the Middle East enters its fourth week, President Trump says the U.S. is considering "winding down" military efforts, as it also seeks to ease the energy crisis by lifting sanctions on Iranian oil stranded at sea.

stories.title: Iran's cultural heritage sites are being damaged by American and Israeli strikes
stories.description: The costs of the joint U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran are mounting in terms of civilian deaths and damage to some of Iran's most famous and beloved cultural heritage sites.

stories.title: 'Everybody was wearing black.' How the Iranian diaspora is observing Nowruz amid war
stories.description: Nowruz celebrates the arrival of spring and rebirth. But for many in the Iranian diaspora, this year is different. As the war continues, many are trying to balance the joy of the holiday with grief.

stories.title: Iranian missiles injure 160 in towns near Israeli nuclear site
stories.description: Israel is investigating how ballistic missiles got through the country's sophisticated air defences.

stories.title: Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran
stories.description: Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground.

stories.title: Why there's no sign of the conflict in the Middle East 'winding down'
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister said that it doesn't appear that the US is prepared to halt its aggression after President Trump said the US was considering "winding down" its war.

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on some Iranian oil as energy prices soar
stories.description: The treasury secretary says the move will quickly bring about 140m barrels of oil to global markets.

stories.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
stories.description: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

stories.title: UK allows US to use bases to strike Strait of Hormuz targets
stories.description: Downing Street says ministers approved the expansion after accusing Iran of "reckless strikes".

stories.title: 'You can't smell Nowruz in the air': Iran marks Persian new year under threat of strikes
stories.description: Iranians speak to the BBC about preparing for the Nowruz new year festival during the war.

stories.title: Are US and Israel aligned on Iran war? Deciphering Trump's post after gas field attacks
stories.description: What does Trump's Truth Social post after gas field attacks tell us about US-Israeli alignment?

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israel 'acted alone' in attack on Iranian gas field
stories.description: Speaking at a news conference, the Israeli leader said Trump had requested that there be no further such attacks.

stories.title: Teenager among first to be executed over Iran's anti-government protests
stories.description: Saleh Mohammadi, a member of Iran's national wrestling team, was found guilty of killing police officers alongside two other men, state media say.

stories.title: Three Palestinian women killed in beauty salon during Iranian missile attack
stories.description: Others were injured when the salon in the southern West Bank was hit, while a Thai worker was killed by falling shrapnel in Israel.

stories.title: 'Very difficult to stop': BBC visits scene of Iran cluster bomb strike on Israel
stories.description: An elderly couple was killed after a bomb flew into their apartment in central Israel and exploded.

stories.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz matters so much in the Iran war
stories.description: Iran says it will "set fire" to ships trying to sail through the world's most vital oil transit point.

stories.title: Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib killed in air strike
stories.description: Israel said it had killed Khatib, whose death was confirmed by Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: The city of Nabatieh in Lebanon has been hit hard by Israeli airstrikes
stories.description: The city of Nabatieh in Lebanon has been hit hard by Israeli airstrikes. "Hezbollah has a strong presence here, this is one of the heartlands of Lebanon's Shia community. Israel says it is targeting fighters who are launching rockets into northern Israel," said FRANCE 24's Claire...

stories.title: Live: Trump threatens to strike Iran’s power plants if Strait of Hormuz does not open within 48 hours
stories.description: President Donald Trump issued Iran with a 48-hour deadline Saturday to re-open the Strait of Hormuz, after which he warned the US will “obliterate” Iranian power plants. Meanwhile, blasts could be heard from Jerusalem on Sunday, after the Israeli military warned of incoming missi...

stories.title: Iran strikes Israeli nuclear town in retaliation for Natanz attack amid escalating conflict
stories.description: An Iranian missile struck the Israeli town of Dimona, home to a nuclear facility, injuring dozens in what Tehran said was retaliation for strikes on its Natanz nuclear site. The attack comes amid escalating regional tensions, including two failed Iranian missile strikes on the US...

stories.title: Iranian missile struck town housing nuclear facility: Iran war shows 'no signs of abating'
stories.description: The iran war is showing "no signs of abating" following a missile strike by Tehran on Dimona, a town in southern Israel housing a nuclear facility, FRANCE 24’s Douglas Herbert said in an analysis. There appears to be no "exit tunnel" in sight with Iran seemingly as determined as ...

stories.title: 🔴 Iranian missile hit town housing nuclear facility, Israeli army says
Trump threatens to send ICE agents to airports amid TSA funding impasse
stories.description: President Donald Trump on Saturday warned he may deploy ICE agents to US airports if Congress fails to fund airport security. The move comes as TSA staff face a second missed paycheck in a 36-day partial government shutdown, raising fears of travel disruption. Critics question IC...

stories.title: War in the Middle East: Strike hits key nuclear site in Iran
stories.description: Hoda Abdel-Hamid is live from Doha, Qatar.

stories.title: Washington sends conflicting signals as war rages on in the Middle East
stories.description: As the Iran conflict enters its fourth week, strikes on nuclear sites and a reported attack on a US-UK base signal further escalation. With no clear exit in sight, fears are mounting of wider instability and humanitarian fallout. “The attack on Diego Garcia is an escalation… it’s...

stories.title: War in the Middle East: Jerusalem Old City targeted by Iranian projectile
stories.description: Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz warned that the United States and Israel would intensify their strikes on Iran in the coming week starting Sunday.

stories.title: Syrian Kurds return home to celebrate Nowruz for the first time since exile
stories.description: Nowruz, the Farsi-language word for “new year,” is an ancient Persian festival that is also celebrate by Kurds in Syria, Turkey and Iraq as well as Iran. It is characterized by colorful street festivals and torch-bearing processions winding their way into the mountains.

stories.title: US to send more Marines to Middle East as Trump hints at wind-down
stories.description: Three weeks into an escalating war in the Middle East, Iran threatened to expand it's retaliatory attacks to include recreational and tourist sites worldwide, as the US announced it was sending more warships and Marines to the region.

stories.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 23 of US-Israel attacks?
stories.description: Trump threatens to hit Iranian energy sites if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened; Tehran vows to retaliate.

stories.title: Drone footage shows damage in Arad after overnight Iranian missile attack
stories.description: Drone footage shows heavy damage to residential buildings in Arad, Israel, after an overnight Iranian missile strike.

stories.title: Trump threatens to ‘obliterate’ Iran power plants unless Hormuz Strait open
stories.description: Donald Trump has threatened to 'obliterate' Iran’s power plants if Tehran fails to open the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: What we know about Iran’s latest attacks on Israel
stories.description: Israel’s air defence system failed to stop at least two Iranian missile strikes on southern Israel.

stories.title: Will the Houthis join Iran in war against Israel and the US?
stories.description: The Yemeni armed group says all options are on the table.

stories.title: Trump issues 48-hour Hormuz Strait ultimatum, threatens Iran’s power plants
stories.description: Iran responds to Trump's threat by saying all US energy infrastructure in the region will be targeted if it is attacked.

stories.title: Iran war live: Trump threatens to attack power plants over Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Donald Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants if it fails to open the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia expels Iran military attache, four embassy staff
stories.description: Move follows attack on Red Sea port of Yanbu, Saudi Arabia's main oil export outlet, after Iran blocked Hormuz Strait.

stories.title: Iran strikes towns near Israel’s nuclear site, wounds over 100
stories.description: Iran targets Dimona and Arad, injuring more than 100 people, in retaliation for an attack on its Natanz nuclear site.

stories.title: State of emergency declared as Iranian missile hits Arad in southern Israel
stories.description: A state of emergency has been declared in Arad, southern Israel after an Iranian missile strike.

stories.title: US says it has crippled Iranian threat in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The US says it hit Iranian coastal missile sites and infrastructure, crippling Tehran’s threat to the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Joe Kent speaks out against Iran war at prayer event after resigning
stories.description: Joe Kent says he resigned as director of the US National Counterterrorism Center over opposition to the war in Iran.

stories.title: EU urges members to start storing winter gas as Iran war causes price surge
stories.description: War, which saw Iran attack Qatar facility, has caused 'high, volatile' gas prices that could hit EU storage projections.

stories.title: Trump threatens Iran with power plant strikes over Hormuz oil blockade
stories.description: US President Donald Trump on Saturday threatened to “obliterate” Iran’s power plants if Tehran does not fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours, a significant escalation barely a day after he talked about “winding down” the war. “If Iran doesn’t fully open, without thre...

stories.title: European nations decry ‘increasing settler terror’ in occupied West Bank
stories.description: Diplomats from 13 European countries and Canada on Saturday slammed growing “terror” by settlers against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank, after a surge in deadly attacks. Since the start of March, six Palestinians have been shot dead in settler attacks in the West Bank, ac...

stories.title: Iran missiles hit southern Israel, injuring more than 100
stories.description: Two Iranian missiles struck southern Israel on Saturday, injuring more than 100 people in the most destructive attack of the three-week war, with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowing to retaliate “on all fronts”. The strikes tore open the facades of residential buildings and ...

stories.title: Democrats push for public debate as US-Israel war on Iran escalates
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Iranian missile strikes on southern Israel and toward a key US-UK military base in the Indian Ocean have escalated the conflict into its fourth week, prompting US Democrats to call for transparency and oversight. The developments are bound to intensify their push in C...

stories.title: Sri Lanka raises fuel prices by 25pc as US-Israel war on Iran bites
stories.description: Sri Lanka raised fuel prices by 25 per cent on Sunday, the second increase in two weeks, as the country prepared for more impact from the war in the Middle East. Regular petrol was increased to 398 rupees ($1.30) per litre, up from 317 rupees, while diesel, the fuel commonly used...

stories.title: 6 killed in Qatar helicopter crash due to technical malfunction, ministry says
stories.description: At least six people have been confirmed dead following the crash of a Qatari military helicopter due to “technical malfunction,” the state’s interior ministry said. In a post on X, the Qatari Interior Ministry announced that six out of seven members on board have been found and c...

stories.title: Trump says considering ‘winding down’ Iran war but rules out ceasefire
stories.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that the United States was considering “winding down” military operations against Iran but was not seeking a ceasefire with the Islamic Republic. Iran’s new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei meanwhile, claimed in a message to mark the first day of...

stories.title: Dubai property sector shows early signs of weakness
stories.description: Dubai’s property market is beginning to show early signs of weakening nearly three weeks into ​the US-Israeli war on Iran, with data from analysts showing tanking transaction volumes and some real estate agents pointing to price reductions. The war, and ‌Tehran’s strikes against ...

stories.title: US stocks skid as Middle East turmoil fans inflation fear
stories.description: Wall Street ended sharply lower on Friday, with the S&P 500 closing at its lowest in six months, as the US-Israeli war against Iran entered its fourth week, deepening worries about inflation and the potential for higher interest rates. The conflict in the Middle East showed no si...

stories.title: Supreme Leader Mojtaba denies Iran’s role in attacks in Oman, Turkiye, dubs them ‘false flag tactic’
stories.description: Iran’s supreme leader, Mojataba Khamenei, denied on Friday Tehran’s role in recent attacks in Turkiye and Oman, claiming that it was a “false flag tactic” by Israel instead. “I should also remark that the attacks against Turkiye and Oman – both of which have good relations with u...

stories.title: Gold falls 1.8pc after report of US sending more troops to Middle East
stories.description: Gold prices fell by 1.8 per cent on Friday as the dollar strengthened on a report that the United States will deploy extra troops in the Middle East, fanning concerns of higher oil prices, inflation, and with it, elevated interest rates. Spot gold fell 1.8pc to $4,563.64 per ounc...

stories.title: Jerusalem’s Muslims despair on Eidul Fitr as access to Al-Aqsa Mosque denied amid US-Israeli war on Iran
stories.description: Hundreds of Muslim worshippers held Eidul Fitr prayers at the gates of Jerusalem’s Old City on Friday, with Israel closing access to the Al-Aqsa mosque and other holy sites over its war on Iran. “Today, Al-Aqsa has been taken from us. It’s a sad and painful Ramazan,” Wajdi Mohamm...

stories.title: US rushes $16bn arms to Gulf after Iran warns of ‘zero restraint’
stories.description: SMOKE rises after an Iranian retaliatory salvo damaged an Israeli oil refinery in Haifa.—Reuters • Tehran strikes Qatari LNG plant, Saudi and Kuwaiti refineries• Trump warns of ‘furious response’ if attacks on Qatar continue• Rules out troop deployment, but officials say reinforc...

stories.title: War Diary Day 21: Muted Nowruz, Eid in Iran
stories.description: On the twenty-first day of the US-Israeli war on Iran, a sombre Nowruz and Eidul Fitr eve set the domestic mood in Iran, as fresh military developments and a shifting coalition posture pointed to an imminent widening of the war. Across Iran, the Persian New Year arrived without u...

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
